steps.title: Contrast study scope
steps.content: BHS needs extensive funding because it is longitudinal: it collects data over long timescales and at intervals that support trend-based conclusions. A more constrained study seeking a single average is less dependent on that kind of funding if it does not need long-term trend tracking.

steps.title: Remove funding distractions
steps.content: Choice B discusses whether scope can expand, not why less funding is needed. Choice C focuses on a possible participant count, not the study's time-based design. Choice D says the study may be a lower funding priority, but the sentence is about dependence on funding. The answer is A.
